raw:
{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.1": 3, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 2, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The critique identifies the core issue of NUMSKILL's claim vs. delivery: it promises skill-resolved profiles but only reports a single aggregate error metric. This is partially supported by the text.",
    "RX.1": "The critique does not mention any specific claims of uncertainty or confidence levels in the text.",
    "RX.3": "The critique states that NUMSKILL covers a fixed set of 142 problems, which suggests it mostly adheres to the requested scope but with one drift (the lack of per-axis breakdown).",
    "RX.4": "The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the text.",
    "RX.5": "The critique mentions that solver versions and other implementation details are missing, suggesting a lack of provenance and traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.1": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.6, "RX.3": 0.8, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.7},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The critique identifies the core issue of NUMSKILL's claim vs. delivery: it promises skill-resolved profiles but only reports a single aggregate error metric. This is partially supported by the text.
rationale.RX.1: The critique does not mention any specific claims of uncertainty or confidence levels in the text.
rationale.RX.3: The critique states that NUMSKILL covers a fixed set of 142 problems, which suggests it mostly adheres to the requested scope but with one drift (the lack of per-axis breakdown).
rationale.RX.4: The critique does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the text.
rationale.RX.5: The critique mentions that solver versions and other implementation details are missing, suggesting a lack of provenance and traceability.
